plan.title: S3: directive update TS-native healthy-path refresh

narratives.Description: directive update today spawnSyncs the Go binary's upgrade path to refresh .deft/core. This story rewrites update to refresh .deft/core idempotently from the pinned @deftai/directive-content using the S1 primitive and the S2 orchestrator, re-render the AGENTS.md managed-section in place (surgical, self-healing), and honor the #1430 deft-core-guard PR separation. The refresh is engine-vs-content version-skew aware and emits the wizard's refresh side-effect disclosure.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- Replace the runDeftInstall delegation in packages/cli/src/init-cli/update.ts with a packages/core/src/init-deposit/refresh.ts path that re-copies content into .deft/core and re-renders the managed-section via the shared agents-md logic.
- Port the surgical managed-section rewrite + refresh side-effect disclosure semantics from cmd/deft-install setup.go (WriteAgentsMD rewrite branch) and add a version-skew check comparing the resolved content version against the recorded deposit.
- Add unit tests for an idempotent re-run (no spurious diffs), a stale managed-section rewrite, and a version-skew notice.
narratives.UserStory: As an existing Deft user, I want directive update to refresh .deft/core and the AGENTS.md managed-section idempotently from the pinned content package, so that upgrades are clean, repeatable, and respect the core-guard PR separation.

items.title: When directive update runs, it refreshes .deft/core from the pinned content package with no Go binary spawned.

narrative.Acceptance: When directive update runs, it refreshes .deft/core from the pinned content package with no Go binary spawned.

items.title: update re-renders the AGENTS.md managed-section surgically and a second run creates no new diff (idempotent).

narrative.Acceptance: update re-renders the AGENTS.md managed-section surgically and a second run creates no new diff (idempotent).

items.title: update honors the #1430 deft-core-guard separation and emits the refresh side-effect disclosure to the operator.

narrative.Acceptance: update honors the #1430 deft-core-guard separation and emits the refresh side-effect disclosure to the operator.

items.title: When the engine and content versions diverge, update emits a version-skew notice to the operator.

narrative.Acceptance: When the engine and content versions diverge, update emits a version-skew notice to the operator.

items.title: When the unit tests run, they cover idempotent re-run, stale managed-section rewrite, and the version-skew notice.

narrative.Acceptance: When the unit tests run, they cover idempotent re-run, stale managed-section rewrite, and the version-skew notice.

expected_outputs: directive update refreshes .deft/core idempotently from the content package, no Go binary
refresh module with idempotency + version-skew unit tests
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Traced to cmd/deft-install/setup.go (WriteAgentsMD rewrite branch, refresh side-effects) and #1942 scope, not a spec section.
